name: Composite Stiffness (Rule of Mixtures)
description: The rule of mixtures predicts the elastic stiffness of a continuous-fiber composite from constituent properties and volume fractions: loading parallel to the fibers obeys the iso-strain Voigt bound while transverse loading obeys the iso-stress Reuss bound, with corrections for fiber orientation giving the effective modulus.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Matrix + fiber properties"]
    N2["Set volume fractions"]
    N3{"Loading direction?"}
    N4["Iso-strain (longitudinal)"]
    N5["Ec = Vf·Ef + Vm·Em"]
    N6["Iso-stress (transverse)"]
    N7["1/Ec = Vf/Ef + Vm/Em"]
    N8["Correct for orientation"]
    N9["Predicted stiffness"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|longitudinal| N4
    N4 --> N5
    N3 -->|transverse| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N5 --> N8
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
